learning_outcomes.description: Examine community profiles and social determinants of health in Australia and globally.
Describe contemporary theories of human development as they relate to lifespan development.
Work in teams to discuss and apply principles of health promotion and ill health prevention across the lifespan and in different contexts.
Identify key priorities in promoting and preventing ill health across the lifespan stages.
Apply principles of health promotion and illness prevention to lifespan stages.

assessments.due_date:
19/08/2026 - 21/08/2026

The exam is due on the 21st August at 1pm.
assessments.task_description:
This assessment is designed to assess knowledge and application of the principles of health, health promotion and human development. All this content is available in Module 1 "Key Foundations" and Lecture 1.

Students will have one (1) opportunity to complete the assessment during the time period.


Personal Laptop/Desktop computer

  • For this assessment you will require a personal laptop or desktop computer that meets the device requirements for Inspera assessments. It is important that you check that your personal laptop or desktop computer meets the device requirements for using the Inspera Assessment platform. If you do not own a suitable laptop, you canﾠborrow one from the Library.
  • Find more information about using Inspera Assessment from the Library website.ﾠGet familiar with Inspera, which provides the latest information for students about using Inspera.


Students are to refer to the course Blackboard site for additional details related to this assessment.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The Online Exam will only be available in Inspera Assessment. Students can commence the Online Exam within the timeframe but will only have one (1) opportunity to complete the Online Exam within the timeframe.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
In this assessment you will critically analyse a selected community using the Australian Bureau of Statistics (ABS) census data, evaluate how a chosen social determinant of health (SDoH) impacts population health outcomes, and apply relevant nursing frameworks to propose evidence-based health promotion strategies appropriate to a specific lifespan group.

Students are to refer to the course Blackboard site for additional details related to this assessment.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students are required to submit an electronic version of their assignment via Blackboard. Please check the companion website assessment area for specific instructions on this process. A link will be made available in the assessment area of your Blackboard course to allow you to submit your assignment electronically.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
12/10/2026 - 28/10/2026

Buddy Check Formative Peer Review: Week 9 Friday @ 1pm 25/09/2025 (no marks attached to this)

Social media reel/post and planning sheet: Week 10 Friday @ 1pm 09/10/2025

Buddy Check Summative Peer Review: Week 10 Friday @ 1pm 09/10/2025

Presentation: Week 11 or 13 during IBL class time
assessments.mode: Activity/ Performance, Oral, Product/ Artefact/ Multimedia, Written
assessments.task_description:
This is a group assessment and will require you to work in groups of 4-6 students. The groups will be provided with a range of lifespan topics related to health promotion and illness prevention. As a group you will:

1.   Create a social media post aimed at a specific lifespan group, focusing on a chosen health topic.

2.   Explain the behind the scenes: Rationale & Research for the social media post including planning sheet.

3.   Explain the behind the scenes: Rationale & Research for the live engagement activity including planning sheet.

4.   Run a live engagement activity.

5.  Provide a wrap-up & key message.

Complete the formative and summative buddy check online assessment.


Students are to refer to the course Blackboard site for additional details related to this assessment.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students are required to submit an electronic version of their assignment via Blackboard. Please check the companion website assessment area for specific instructions on this process. A link will be made available in the assessment area of your Blackboard course to allow you to submit your assignment electronically.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 3(27 Jul - 16 Aug)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 1 - Key Foundations

This module forms the basis of this course. It will cover understanding definitions of health and determinants of health, health promotion and human development.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 1 - Key Foundations This module forms the basis of this course. It will cover understanding definitions of health and determinants of health, health promotion and human development.

learning_activities.topic:
Lecture One - Week 1

This lecture will introduce you to the course and assessments.
We will also look at different lifespan stages and the determinants of health for each stage.


Learning outcomes: L01
learning_activities.topic_description: Lecture One - Week 1 This lecture will introduce you to the course and assessments. We will also look at different lifespan stages and the determinants of health for each stage.

learning_activities.topic:
IBL 1 - Week 3

Your first IBL will introduce you to different community profiles and the social determinants of health. This will help with Assessment 1 and 2. You will also form groups for Assessment 3.

NOTE - This week there is a public holiday for EKKA Day on Wednesday 12th August. If you are allocated a Wednesday tutorial then please check your timetable as tutorials have been reallocated for week 3 only to the Tuesday.

Affected classes include:

Tutorial 5

Tutorial 6

Tutorial 9

Tutorial 10


Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04

learning_activities.topic:
Module 2 - Prenatal to toddlerhood

This module covers prenatal to toddlerhood. It explores developmental progress of each stage, the role of the nurse in assessment as well as health promotion for this lifespan group.


Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04
